relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Morning brings the death-council to decision.
Jesus is bound and handed to Pilate.
Judas sees condemnation and is pierced by regret.
He names innocent blood, but the leaders refuse responsibility.
The silver returns to the temple as Judas collapses.
They call the money blood-price.
The price buys a burial field for strangers.
The field is remembered as blood-marked.
Prophetic memory gathers the thirty silver pieces.
The potter’s field becomes part of fulfillment.
Before Pilate, Jesus answers the king question with You say it.
He remains silent before accusation.
Pilate presses him to hear the charges.
His silence makes the governor marvel.
Festival release becomes the setting of choice.
Barabbas stands as the other prisoner.
The choice is placed before the crowd.
Pilate sees envy beneath the handing-over.
Pilate’s wife is warned by dream about the righteous one.
The leaders persuade the crowd toward Barabbas.
Barabbas is chosen for release.
The crowd calls for Jesus to be crucified.
Pilate asks what harm he has done, but the cry grows.
Pilate washes hands yet remains inside the act.
The crowd takes blood-language onto itself.
Barabbas is released and Jesus handed over.
Soldiers gather around him.
A scarlet cloak turns kingship into mockery.
Thorns crown the mocked king.
The mockery becomes bodily violence.
He is led away to crucifixion.
Simon of Cyrene is compelled to carry the cross.
They reach Golgotha, place of a skull.
He refuses the numbing drink.
His garments are divided by lot.
They sit and keep watch.
The charge above him names him King.
He is crucified between two others.
Passersby mock with shaking heads.
They test sonship through coming down.
Leaders join the mockery.
They cannot understand saving that refuses self-saving spectacle.
Trust in the Divine is thrown back at him.
Even those crucified with him reproach him.
Darkness covers the land.
Jesus prays the psalm of forsakenness aloud.
Some misunderstand the cry as calling Elijah.
Sour wine is lifted toward him.
Others wait to see whether Elijah will come.
Jesus cries again and releases the spirit.
The temple veil tears, earth shakes, and rocks split.
Tombs open and holy ones are raised.
After his rising, they appear in the holy city.
The centurion confesses him as Son of the Divine.
Women who served him watch from afar.
The women witnesses are named.
Joseph of Arimathea comes as disciple.
He asks Pilate for the body.
The body is wrapped in clean linen.
A new rock tomb receives him and a stone is rolled.
Mary Magdalene and the other Mary sit opposite the tomb.
The leaders gather again after Preparation.
They remember the promise of rising after three days.
They seek to secure the tomb against the resurrection claim.
Pilate grants a guard.
Stone, seal, and guard attempt to secure death.
